ideal_answer_summary: Software testing is the process of verifying and validating software to ensure it meets requirements.
Manual testing involves executing test cases without automation tools.
Automated testing uses tools and scripts to execute test cases.
A test case defines steps and expected results to verify functionality.
A bug is a defect causing incorrect or unexpected behavior.
Regression testing ensures new changes do not break existing functionality.
Unit testing verifies individual components of software.
Integration testing checks interaction between modules.
System testing validates the complete integrated system.
Acceptance testing ensures software meets business requirements.
Black box testing tests functionality without knowing internal code.
White box testing examines internal code structure.
Smoke testing verifies basic functionality of new build.
Sanity testing checks specific functionality after small changes.
A test plan outlines testing approach scope and schedule.
A test scenario describes high level functionality to test.
Defect lifecycle tracks bug status from discovery to closure.
Alpha testing is internal testing before external release.
Beta testing is conducted by real users before official launch.
Usability testing checks user friendliness.
Performance testing measures speed and stability under load.
Load testing checks system performance under expected load.
Stress testing evaluates system under extreme conditions.
Automation framework provides structure for test scripts.
API testing validates backend services.
Exploratory testing involves unscripted testing based on intuition.
Boundary value analysis tests input limits.
Equivalence partitioning divides inputs into valid and invalid groups.
Test coverage measures extent of testing performed.
A bug report documents issue details and reproduction steps.
TDD involves writing tests before writing code.
Continuous testing integrates testing in CI CD pipeline.

API automation uses scripts to test services.
Cross browser testing ensures consistent behavior.

Performance metrics measure system responsiveness.

Test environment replicates production setup.
